title: Sleeping in a cellar, waiting at a funeral home, riding the tram: How the French are improvising to survive the heatwave
subhead: Residents describe the sometimes unusual strategies they have adopted to endure the heat. Their improvised solutions highlight the shortcomings in climate change adaptation.

title: Monitoring increases after France reports first diagnosed Ebola case

title: Dayot Upamecano, the leader of the French defense, faces the Haaland menace at the 2026 World Cup
subhead: Upamecano, who plays for Bayern Munich, faces the daunting task of limiting Manchester City striker Erling Haaland's influence on Friday, in the high-stakes match between France and Norway.

title: As Parisians seek heatwave relief, canal swimming proves difficult to supervise
subhead: A section of the Canal Saint-Martin has been open for swimming, under regulated conditions, since June 16. But police and staff are struggling to keep the excitement in check, as a massive influx of young people look to cool off.

title: 250 years of American independence: Thomas Jefferson and John Adams, 'the North and South poles of the American Revolution'
subhead: 'America 250' (4/13). Fifty years to the day after the adoption of the Declaration of Independence, two of its architects died: the enigmatic Thomas Jefferson and the impulsive John Adams. This coincidence only strengthened, among the population, the sense of an unprecedented destiny promised to their nation.

title: Living under Paris's zinc roofs in 40°C heat: 'One day, the people living there will look for those responsible'
subhead: As France faces extreme heat, many of these top-story spaces have become uninhabitable. Some residents have formed groups to call on their landlords, neighbors and local authorities to address their living conditions.
